notes: Spec asserts: ready → working → ready and state never enters waiting. The recipe drives a single auto-executed bash call ('echo hello') followed by a terminal reply. Under bypass_tool_permissions the bash tool auto-runs (no permission dialog), so PermissionPending is never set and waiting is structurally unreachable — the assistant(tool_calls)→tool_result cycle is just continued working activity, and the text-only terminal assistant (turn_done) is the only ready settle. If the recording ever flips to waiting, the bash tool tripped a gate (different feature) and the recipe/settings must be tightened. BACKFLOW (live recording): mistral-vibe is a presession adapter — a transient proc-<PID> row is born ready before the real session_<id> transcript dir appears, then reconciles to a DIFFERENT session_id, so the birth is modeled as two phases (presession_birth → session_birth new_session) templated from the codex sibling, with every post-birth phase (incl. the tool-call turn) anchored same_session_as session_birth. OBSERVATIONS reduced to agent-only (cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false): the offline replay golden stages only transcript.jsonl, so vibe's meta.json sidecar model + tokens are unverifiable offline (same as kiro-cli).

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before the transcript dir exists)
Real session_<id> reconciles (different session_id) when the transcript directory appears, born ready
vibe PID bound to the real session (matched by cwd)
User prompt drives ready → working on the real session (spec bullet 1)
Turn completes back to ready; the bash tool_call ran auto-executed (bypass_tool_permissions) and state never entered waiting (spec bullet 2); final state ready
